title: Medical device
abstract: Provided is a medical device comprising: a solenoid; a permanent magnet having a lumen extending therethrough, the permanent magnet configured to be axially movable within the solenoid; a cannulated shaft configured to run through the lumen of the permanent magnet; an inner tubular member having an open distal end; and an outer tubular member having a distal end and an open window disposed at the distal end, wherein the permanent magnet is configured to reciprocate the cannulated shaft when the solenoid is in electrical communication with a power source, and wherein the open distal end of the inner tubular member and the open window of the outer tubular member are configured to form a cutting tool during an operation of the medical device.
claims: Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A medical device comprising: a solenoid; a permanent magnet having a lumen extending therethrough, the permanent magnet configured to be received within the solenoid; and a cannulated shaft configured to go through the lumen of the permanent magnet, wherein the permanent magnet is configured to drive the cannulated shaft when the solenoid is in electrical communication with a power source. 2. The medical device of claim 1 , wherein the medical device further comprises an inner tubular member having an open distal end. 3. The medical device of claim 2, wherein the inner tubular member is configured to be operably connected to the cannulated shaft. 4. The medical device of claim 2, wherein the medical device further comprises an outer tubular member having a distal end and an open window disposed at the distal end. 5. The medical device of claim 4, wherein the open distal end of the inner tubular member and the open window of the outer tubular member are configured to form a cutting tool during an operation of the medical device. 6. The medical device of claim 5, wherein the outer tubular member is configured to be stationary. 7. The medical device of claim 5, wherein the outer tubular member is configured to remain stationary relative to the inner tubular member. 8. The medical device of claim 1 , wherein the permanent magnet is a ring or tubular magnet. 9. The medical device of claim 1 , wherein the solenoid is configured to be stationary. 10. The medical device of claim 1 , wherein the medical device further comprises a housing. 11. A medical device comprising: a solenoid; a permanent magnet having a lumen extending therethrough, the permanent magnet configured to be axially movable within the solenoid; a cannulated shaft configured to run through the lumen of the permanent magnet; and a working member configured to be operably connected to the cannulated shaft, wherein the cannulated shaft is configured to drive the working member when the solenoid is in electrical communication with a power source. 12. The medical device of claim 11 , wherein the working member comprises an outer tubular member and an inner tubular member. 13. The medical device of claim 12, wherein the inner tubular member has an open distal end and the outer tubular member has an open window. 14. The medical device of claim 13, wherein the open distal end of the inner tubular member and the open window of the outer tubular member are configured to form a cutting tool during an operation of the medical device. 15. The medical device of claim 12, wherein the inner tubular member is configured to be operably connected to the cannulated shaft. 16. A medical device comprising: a handle; a solenoid disposed inside the handle; a permanent magnet having a lumen extending therethrough, the permanent magnet configured to be axially movable within the solenoid; and a cannulated shaft configured to run through the lumen of the permanent magnet; an inner tubular member having an open distal end; and an outer tubular member having a distal end and an open window disposed at the distal end, wherein the permanent magnet is configured to drive the inner tubular member when the solenoid is in electrical communication with a power source. 17. The medical device of claim 16, wherein the open distal end of the inner tubular member and the open window of the outer tubular member are configured to form a cutting tool during an operation of the medical device. 18. The medical device of claim 16, wherein the inner tubular member is configured to be operably connected to the cannulated shaft. 19. The medical device of claim 16, wherein the outer tubular member is configured to remain stationary relative to the inner tubular member. 20. The medical device of claim 16, wherein the outer tubular member is configured to be stationary.
